page: Amazon Web Services m5.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8175M processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 33 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is consistently weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tasks. Conversely, multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached reads and writes also falls within the average range. The instance achieves average throughput in database operations, Redis SET operations at 13.15 million operations per second, and static web serving at 11.92 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require steady multi-core execution rather than peak single-threaded speed. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for workloads relying on network-attached storage. This hardware profile is appropriate for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, database operations, and moderate software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it operates at 3.1 GHz and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff, with weak single-core execution but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The instance delivers average throughput for database operations, Redis SET tasks, and static web serving. Qualitatively, its balanced resource allocation offers steady multi-threaded performance without the premium cost of specialized compute or memory-optimized hardware. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database hosting, and multi-threaded application processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 RAM, and average multi-core performance capabilities.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server offering dedicated multi-core processing and steady memory bandwidth for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB DDR4 RAM
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Weak single-core performance relative to higher-tier instances
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Suitable for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
